title: [BUG] saxes wants: {"node":">=10"}, but exceljs wants: {"node":">=8"}
author: Tilogorn
body:
## 🐛 Bug Report

According to `package.json`, exceljs [still supports Node.js version 8](https://github.com/exceljs/exceljs/blob/master/package.json#L16). But installing a version greather than `3.8.0`, where `sax` was replaced by `saxes` throws this warning:

```
$ npm install exceljs@3.8.1
npm WARN notsup Unsupported engine for saxes@5.0.1: wanted: {"node":">=10"} (current: {"node":"8.17.0","npm":"6.14.4"})
npm WARN notsup Not compatible with your version of node/npm: saxes@5.0.1
```

Lib version: 3.8.1

## Steps To Reproduce

1. Install nodejs version 8
2. run `$ npm install exceljs`

## The expected behaviour:

When the library supports Node.js version 8, its dependencies should also.

## Possible solutions
- Drop support for Node.js version 8 by exceljs: easy but :frowning_face:
- Reverting `saxes` to `sax`: possibly no option due to the work already done for the dep change
- Asking the maintainer of saxes if he really does not support Node.js version 8 (and if he supports it, revert the change in his `package.json`)
- Using an older version of saxes@v4.0.2 which supports Node.js version 8

comments.author: alubbe
comments.body:
Argh, this really sucks, because our npm package actually includes a fully built bundle that is node v8 compatible.
So unfortunately, we can't go back to `sax`, we can't use `saxes@v4.0.2` because we need the streaming features from v5 and `saxes@v5` is using modern concepts that requires node v10+. 

So... I'm not sure about what to do here. The truth is you don't need any dependencies at all when you're using our pre-built ES5 bundles (which is what you'll want for node v8 going forward, as we start using async iterators in exceljs v4). See https://github.com/exceljs/exceljs#es5-imports

@Siemienik @guyonroche should we maybe publish our ES5 files directly to npm, as different a package, with 0 dependencies? That would make for a very simple import for those users that need that ES5 version.

comments.author: alubbe
comments.body:
wait actually - how does it break for you but it's green on our CI?
https://github.com/exceljs/exceljs/runs/630211260
https://github.com/exceljs/exceljs/runs/630211271
https://github.com/exceljs/exceljs/runs/630211281

ah, you're just reporting a warning. Yeah I think you can ignore it - or have you actually had any errors?

comments.author: Tilogorn
comments.body:
Hi @alubbe 

yes, I'm just reporting a warning and I could not identify any errors on my side. But maintaining a legacy codebase in Node.js 8 taught me to pay attention to such warnings ;-)

The pre-built bundle as a different package seems to be a nice idea to me. Would that be possible?

If not, could you document the API methods that use the Node.js 10 features so I can avoid them? The application heavily depends on streaming, so I guess there might be some overlapping. Or do you provide fallbacks for Node.js 8? "Yeah I think you can ignore it" is a not-so-reliable statement to ship this to productive running systems using Node.js 8.

comments.author: alubbe
comments.body: totally understand that. from our end, our full test suite is passing, so we would consider it safe. if that's not enough, please open a similar ticket with saxes and ask them what specifically might not work with node v8.
